item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather boring and makes no sense. No explanation is ever-slightly suggested to anything. Requires purpose and rewrite. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/topbunk/8461089299/
Author: Chris Hanson
Licence: CC BY 2.0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something iffy about the way this one is written, and I don't like it at all. I would suggest a full rewrite. Downvoted

item.title: Sorry I've been away so long...
item.content:encoded: I've been rather busy getting our new department up and running. I will be back from time to time, but I don't know how much time I will be able to devote to the SCPs. As for your question, consider the source of the anomaly. It is a doll, manufactured sometime during the Victorian era. If you are unfamiliar with the cultural norms of that era, I will explain briefly. Women were expected to behave as fragile creatures, prone to fainting (which was more due to the impossibly tight corsets they were expected to wear than anything else) and not mentally advanced enough to handle anything other than basic housework. I'm NOT condoning it, I am simply pointing out that the doll is creating the atmosphere that it is familiar with. Girls of that era were expected to be "girly." That is what the doll creates, an atmosphere conducive to what *it* expects a "proper" girl to be attracted to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed- this is just my theory.

It was still able to transform the female subjects it didn't like, which apparently males are immune to.

They don't know that males are immune for that matter; just that it hasn't ever affected one. It might even be dangerous to mess too much with its simplistic view of male and female- suppose they accidentally enlighten it to the complexity of gender roles, thus rendering males valid candidates?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thinking is that its actually a matter of the doll1's perception

I think that this is significant enough that the article should be less ambiguous about it.

It rejected two potential candidates that it apparently didn't like, but found the third acceptable.

It was still able to transform the female subjects it didn't like, which apparently males are immune to.

If it decided it liked or didn't like a trans-female or a male who presented themselves as a potential "playmate", they would probably be affected just like any other candidate

If this is untested then it remain possible and the article shouldn't discount it without evidence. Which it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thinking is that its actually a matter of the doll1's perception, rather than any other factor. It rejected two potential candidates that it apparently didn't like, but found the third acceptable. If it decided it liked or didn't like a trans-female or a male who presented themselves as a potential "playmate", they would probably be affected just like any other candidate. A female not expressing interest at all might upset it, or it might ignore them as a non-factor. As it stands, however, those presenting as male are talkative furniture to it by default, while it seems to consider those presenting as females viable candidates by default.

Footnotes
1. by which I mean the potentially sentient whatever-the-hell-this-thing-really-is that turns people into tea pots

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2116-1 must be a female subject, as males seem to be unaffected by the anomalous nature of SCP-2116

This bugs me enough that I am downvoting. Is this based on gender identity, presence of sex chromosomes or what? What about transgender, intersex, and non-binary persons? Why is a male person who collects Victorian dolls and tea sets immune, but a female person who eschews western models of femininity like that not is immune? This seens like an important detail about how this functions and the ommision isn't even lampshaded here.

Would it affect a woman who has no cultural reference to this stuff?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think that this could change the entire world into tea party mania! That would be so silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this article, and upvoted. I like the idea, what if this was uncontained? The whole world would become a little girl's tea party.

However I believe the article can be improved further.

Personally, I think it would be more clear if -1 only referred to a successful subject, with failed subjects not being referred to as -1.

Nitpicking, possible issues which could be fixed
Nitpicking, possible issues which could be fixed

The Foundation has listed extensive criteria for the selection of a new -1 … Despite having only one successful -1, and 2 failed subjects. And the current -1 has held the position for 2 months.
Fixing this might be as simple as stating that the only successful -1 had these qualities, and the Foundation is hoping that they are appropriate criteria.

Also it is weird that everyone assumed the girls are 11, when most people would probably round to 10, unless it is and effect of the object.

"deceased elderly female"
Does this mean a photograph of a dead body? That is a thing people used to do, take pictures of deceased family members. If the woman in the photo is not dead in the photo, the word deceased could be dropped.

That is just my opinion though. Like I said, I allready upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Updated just for you. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't, not once, say what SCP-2116-1 is. It's implied what it is, but it's never actually spelled out. What if a new guy was assigned to this? He'd be wasting time by asking what -1 is.

Speaking of -1, that's the rating I'm going to give this for now, because that just peeves me off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as you know they have nothing to fear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly. Heheheheheheh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I really appreciate that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just occurred to me that, other than the fact that it looks creepy, guys have nothing to fear from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: <Will whack MadCatUSA with an SCP as soon as I write one>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it when someone(in this case me) finds a strange sort of "thread" running through an SCP and it turns out that it was intentional. Don't get me wrong, SCPs that accidentally show you something deeper than what it seems to be are all well and good. But there's nothing quite like the joy in finding the real deal and the author confirming it. Good show, man.

This would've been a good article had you just stopped withe test just before the last one, but the last one is where all those pieces fell together juuuuust right and kicked it into "AWESOME" territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I was dubious (creepy dolls being so so so common) but I enjoyed it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exactly what I was going for. Thank you for seeing that. :-)
I read a while back what Zyn had written about not basing a SCP off an object but rather finding an emotion and going from there. I thought about lonlieness and what a doll might feel if its owner abandoned it. The rest came surprisingly easily. It only took me about 20-30 minutes to write and didn't require all that much editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, as creepy as this is…it isn't that bad. What I mean is, it seems like it just wants a purpose. And a doll's purpose is to be played with. It apparently even asked the latest "victim" if she was okay with what was going to happen. Why else would she have said "yes"? +1 for being creepy and for having surprisingly sympathetic motivation. What it does is horrifying. But it's horrifying in the way a child who's killed someone is. They genuinely don't know they've done anything wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anyone else points things out, I will look at editing. Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Extra details on the nature and role of SCP-2116-1 and SCP-2116's effects on living things mainly. Seems to only be a problem to me, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heheheheeheh… <slips doll under Fae's bed in the middle of the night…>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good! It's always nice to see things that have been done really well in the past be done again really well now.
The test subject sections were also really well done, there really aren't enough creepy transformations on the website, if the doll had just killed those people it wouldn't have been as interesting as you made it. Nice job again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They might not, if you can't bring a modern camera into its field of effect without it becoming a daguerreotype camera. What you're seeing (the darkened corners and odd focus) is either the result of a fairly good photographer using an actual daguerreotype camera or pinhole camera projecting a round image onto a square plate, or a decent fake-up of that look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would kill the first sentence and just start it with "Tables will be set…"

I'll watch feedback and consider it. Thanks for the input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What kind of girl? How many girls like Victoriana?

Consider when the doll was manufactured.

So if the item is unpossessed for a year…we're dealing with a Keter class object. Can it cause a reality-bending K-Class event over the whole world?

Hehehehehehehehehehe….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels like a lot of details about the doll that would normally be delegated to the description are scattered across the other sections of the article.

Could you provide some details please? Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The descriptions of the "failed" test subjects were terrible to read, and I enjoyed every part of it. A solid, creepy entry all around: +1!

My only real problem is with:

The environment, decor, ambient temperature, and furnishings become altered to be more attractive to a pre-pubescent human female. Tables will be set with a pink tea set with a teapot filled with a sweet red liquid, various sweet treats will manifest, and the table cover will become a lace tablecloth of a style popular during the mid-19th Century.

The first part is just way too over-reaching (and highly subjective) a statement. For example, I can't imagine that a young girl growing up in the poorest parts of Ethiopia, one living the high-life on 5th Avenue, and one from uh, darkest Peru would have that same ideal atheistic. I would kill the first sentence and just start it with "Tables will be set…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
furnishings become altered to be more attractive to a pre-pubescent human female

What kind of girl? How many girls like Victoriana? Which incidentally wouldn't feature pink things if we're being historically accurate. How many more would like to live in the undersea home of Spongebob Squarepants? Could the Foundation have experimented with girls from different backgrounds?

For every hour that SCP-2116 is not in the possession of SCP-2116-1, the region of altered reality expands by 1 meter

So if the item is unpossessed for a year…we're dealing with a Keter class object. Can it cause a reality-bending K-Class event over the whole world?

The subject, now designated SCP-2116-1

it might get confusing, having different -1s at different times. Use SCP-2116-1-1, SCP-2116-1-2 etc?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like this, but the picture has some sort of filter, and I feel the Foundation would have a scientifical, clean, picture. But +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels like a lot of details about the doll that would normally be delegated to the description are scattered across the other sections of the article. When applied correctly, this kind of thing can work in favor of an article, but I don't think it works here. A bit of a confusing read initially, for me at least. Can't say I'm too crazy about the object itself either. Sorry, but this just isn't my thing.

Also,

The environment, decor, ambient temperature, and furnishings become altered to be more attractive to a pre-pubescent human female.

maybe instead say something like "altered to what is seen as typically attractive to pre-pubescent girls" because not all girls are going to enjoy the same things by default.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A this point, I can safely say that this is terrific. Also, keep that thing faaaaar away from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time I think there's no more ways to do a good creepy doll, somebody goes and makes me upvote another one…
